Title: Crawl4AI: Arbitrary file write (path traversal) in crawler downloads can lead to RCE
Description: When the crawler saves a downloaded file, the destination filename was taken from attacker-influenced input and joined to the downloads directory with no confinement. A filename containing an absolute path (e.g. `/etc/cron.d/evil`) or `../` traversal escaped the downloads directory, giving an arbitrary file write with attacker-controlled contents. Because the written bytes are attacker-controlled, this escalates to remote code execution (overwriting a shell rc-file, `~/.ssh/authorized_keys`, a cron entry, or a Python module on the import path).
